description: A WhatsApp-like mobile app for storing and sharing valuable memories of children with trusted family members

phases.name: Project Foundation & Development Environment
objectives: Establish React Native project with TypeScript
Configure development tooling and CI/CD pipeline
Ensure app builds and runs on both iOS and Android
deliverables: App successfully builds on iOS simulator/device
App successfully builds on Android emulator/device
CI pipeline runs tests and linting
README with setup instructions
tests: Jest smoke test verifying app renders
Lint and format checks pass in CI

phases.name: Chat UI Skeleton (No Backend)
objectives: Build WhatsApp-like chat interface with mock data
Implement chat list and individual chat screens
Create message rendering for all media types
features: Chat list screen showing multiple chats with preview
Individual chat screen with message history
Message bubble rendering (text, audio placeholder, photo, video)
Message input UI with send button
Mock data structure for chats and messages
Navigation between chat list and chat detail
deliverables: Fully navigable chat UI with mock data
WhatsApp-like visual design and interactions
Responsive layout for different screen sizes
tests: Snapshot tests for chat list and chat screens
RTL tests for message rendering states

phases.name: Local Persistence & Offline-First Architecture
objectives: Implement local SQLite database for chats and messages
Build offline message queue with automatic retry
Handle connectivity state changes gracefully
features: Local SQLite schema for chats, messages, and queue
Message send queue with pending/sent/failed states
Optimistic UI updates for sent messages
deliverables: Messages persist locally across app restarts
Offline messages queue and retry automatically
UI reflects message send status accurately

phases.name: Media Capture & Local Processing
objectives: Enable photo, video, and audio capture/selection
Implement local media compression and caching
Build media upload queue infrastructure
features: Photo capture from camera or gallery selection
Video capture from camera or gallery selection
Upload ORIGINAL full-quality media (no compression on upload)
Generate and cache compressed local copy for playback
Media upload queue with retry logic and resumable uploads
Media preview in message bubbles using compressed local copy
Re-download original high-quality asset on demand
Storage of both original and compressed file metadata
deliverables: Users can attach photos, videos, and audio to messages
Original full-quality uploaded to R2, compressed copy cached locally
Upload queue handles failures and retries
Users can re-download original quality at any time
tests: Compression quality tests for local cache

phases.name: Backend Foundation & Database Schema
objectives: Set up NodeJS/Express backend with Clerk authentication
Design and implement Neon Postgres schema with Drizzle ORM
Create core API endpoints for chats and messages
features: Drizzle schema for users, chats, chat_members, messages, media_attachments
CRUD endpoints for chats (create, list, get)
CRUD endpoints for messages (create, list, get)
deliverables: Backend API running and accessible
Database schema deployed to Neon
API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)
Authenticated endpoints functional
tests: API integration tests for all endpoints
Access control tests for different roles

tables: users (id, clerk_user_id, email, display_name, revenuecat_customer_id, subscription_tier, created_at, updated_at)
chats (id, name, created_by, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at)
chat_members (id, chat_id, user_id, role, joined_at, left_at)
messages (id, chat_id, sender_id, content, type, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at)
media_attachments (id, message_id, original_storage_key, compressed_storage_key, mime_type, original_size_bytes, compressed_size_bytes, width, height, duration_seconds, created_at)
invites (id, chat_id, created_by, token, expires_at, max_uses, used_count, created_at)
public_share_links (id, media_attachment_id, share_token, created_by, expires_at, access_count, revoked_at, created_at)
usage_tracking (id, user_id, period_start, period_end, messages_sent, media_uploaded_mb, storage_used_mb, created_at, updated_at)

phases.name: Cloudflare R2 Media Storage Integration
objectives: Configure Cloudflare R2 bucket for high-quality media storage
Implement presigned URL generation for secure uploads
Build media upload, retrieval, and public sharing endpoints
Support re-download of original quality assets
features: R2 bucket configuration with CORS and lifecycle rules
Store both original and compressed file metadata in Postgres (file_size, mime_type, dimensions, duration)
Presigned URL endpoint for authenticated downloads (GET /api/media/:id/download-url?quality=original|compressed)
Public share link generation with time-limited access (POST /api/media/:id/share)
Time-limited presigned URLs for public sharing (configurable expiration: 1-24 hours)
File type and size validation (preserve original quality)
Track share link usage and analytics
deliverables: Mobile app uploads original full-quality media to R2 via presigned URLs
Users can download original or compressed versions
Users can generate time-limited public share links
Public share links expire automatically after configured duration
Media metadata (original + compressed) tracked in database
tests: Presigned URL generation tests (upload and download)
Upload flow integration tests (original quality preservation)
Download URL access control tests (authenticated vs public)
Public share link generation and expiration tests

phases.name: Authentication & User Management
objectives: Integrate Clerk authentication in mobile app
Implement sign-in flows (email, Google, Apple)
Build user profile with free plan usage tracking
features: Clerk SDK integration in React Native (authentication only)
User profile screen with usage statistics
Free plan usage display (messages sent, storage used, limits)
Usage progress bars and visual indicators
Auth token management for API calls
deliverables: Users can sign up and sign in via all methods
Authentication state persists across app restarts
API calls include valid auth tokens
Unauthenticated users redirected to sign-in
Profile displays current free plan usage and limits

phases.name: RevenueCat Integration & Subscription Management
objectives: Integrate RevenueCat for in-app purchases
Implement subscription tiers (Free, Premium)
Build paywall and subscription management UI
features: RevenueCat SDK integration in React Native
Configure offerings and products in RevenueCat dashboard
Free tier with usage limits (e.g., 100 messages/month, 500MB storage)
Paywall UI triggered when limits reached
RevenueCat webhook handler for subscription events
Usage enforcement based on subscription tier
deliverables: Users can purchase Premium subscription via in-app purchase
Users see usage limits in profile
Premium users have extra storage space and unlimited message count
Subscription status synced between RevenueCat and backend
Paywall appears when free tier limits reached

phases.name: Invite System (QR Codes & Links)
objectives: Implement invite generation with QR codes and shareable links
Build invite acceptance flow
Support cross-country invitations via links
features: Generate invite tokens with expiration and usage limits
QR code generation for in-person invites
Shareable invite link generation (pipo://invite/TOKEN or https://pipo.app/invite/TOKEN)
QR code scanner in mobile app
Deep link handling for invite links
Invite acceptance flow (join chat as member)
Share invite link via system share sheet
deliverables: Users can generate QR codes for chat invites
Users can generate and share invite links
Recipients can scan QR or click link to join chat
Invite tokens validated and tracked server-side
tests: Invite token generation and validation tests

phases.name: Chat Roles & Access Control
objectives: Implement role-based permissions (Owner, Admin, Member)
Enforce access control on all operations
Build member management UI
features: Role assignment on chat creation (creator = Owner)
Role assignment on invite acceptance (default = Member)
Permission matrix enforcement (who can invite, remove, delete messages, etc.)
Member list UI in chat settings
deliverables: Role-based permissions enforced on all operations
Owners and Admins can manage members
Removed users lose access immediately
UI reflects user's role and permissions
tests: Permission enforcement tests for each role

phases.name: Real-time Sync & Message Delivery
objectives: Implement real-time message synchronization
Build efficient polling or WebSocket mechanism
Sync messages across chat members

realtime: WebSockets (ws or Socket.io) or Server-Sent Events

features: WebSocket connection for real-time updates (or polling)
Message delivery to all chat members
deliverables: Messages appear in real-time for all chat members
Offline messages sync when connectivity restored
No duplicate messages in UI

phases.name: Daily Digest Push Notifications
objectives: Implement daily digest notifications at 9:00 PM local time
Ensure generic notifications with no message previews
Build notification scheduling system
features: Daily digest scheduler (runs at 9:00 PM user local time)
Generic notification message (no chat titles or previews)
Deep link from notification to app
deliverables: Users receive one daily digest at 9:00 PM if there was activity
Notifications are generic with no sensitive content
Users can enable/disable notifications

phases.name: Public Media Sharing & High-Quality Downloads
objectives: Build UI for generating public share links with time-limited access
Implement high-quality media re-download functionality
Enable sharing memories with non-app users
features: Media detail view with sharing options
Generate public share link UI (configurable expiration: 1, 6, 12, 24 hours)
Share link via system share sheet (WhatsApp, email, SMS, etc.)
View active share links for a media item
Download original quality button in media viewer
Download progress indicator for high-quality downloads
Local storage management (delete compressed cache, keep original)
Public share link landing page (web view for non-app users)
Share link analytics (view count, last accessed)
Quality selector UI (compressed for playback, original for download/share)
deliverables: Users can generate time-limited public share links for any media
Share links work for non-app users via web browser
Users can re-download original quality at any time
Share links expire automatically after configured duration
Users can manage and revoke active share links

phases.name: Soft Delete & 30-Day Retention
objectives: Implement soft-delete for messages and media
Build 30-day retention and hard-delete system
Align R2 lifecycle with retention policy
features: Soft-delete flag (deleted_at timestamp) on messages and chats
Restore functionality for soft-deleted content (within 30 days)
Hard-delete job runs daily to purge content older than 30 days
R2 lifecycle rule to delete media after 30 days of soft-delete
Audit log for deletions and restorations
deliverables: Deleted messages/chats soft-deleted and hidden
Content restorable within 30-day window
Hard-delete automatically purges after 30 days
R2 media cleaned up in sync with database

phases.name: Security Hardening & Encryption
objectives: Implement encryption at rest and in transit
Ensure compliance with 'standard protection' security model
Conduct security audit and penetration testing

testing: OWASP ZAP or similar security testing tools

features: Neon database encryption at rest enabled
R2 bucket encryption at rest enabled
API rate limiting to prevent abuse
CSRF protection for web endpoints (if any)
deliverables: All data encrypted in transit (TLS)
All data encrypted at rest (Neon + R2)
API protected against common vulnerabilities
Security audit report with remediation

phases.name: Performance Optimization & Caching
objectives: Optimize app performance for smooth UX
Implement caching strategies for media and data
Reduce API latency and improve load times
features: API response caching with React Query
deliverables: App loads and scrolls smoothly
Media loads quickly with thumbnails
API response times under acceptable thresholds
No memory leaks or performance regressions

phases.name: Error Handling & User Feedback
objectives: Implement comprehensive error handling
Provide clear user feedback for all states
Build retry and recovery mechanisms
features: Global error boundary in React Native
Loading states for all async operations
Empty states for lists and screens
Network error handling and offline mode messaging
deliverables: All errors handled gracefully with user feedback
Users can retry failed operations
Crashes logged and reported for debugging
Clear loading and empty states throughout app

phases.name: Testing & Quality Assurance
objectives: Achieve comprehensive test coverage
Conduct end-to-end testing on real devices
Perform user acceptance testing (UAT)
features: Unit tests for all business logic
Component tests for all UI components
E2E tests for critical user flows
deliverables: Test coverage above 80% for critical paths
All E2E tests passing on CI
UAT completed with stakeholder approval
Bug backlog triaged and prioritized
tests: Full test suite execution in CI
E2E test scenarios for all major flows

phases.name: Beta Release & Feedback Loop
objectives: Deploy beta version to TestFlight (iOS) and Google Play Beta (Android)
Gather user feedback and analytics
Iterate based on real-world usage
features: Beta app distribution via TestFlight and Google Play Beta
Analytics tracking for key user actions
User survey for feature requests and pain points
deliverables: Beta app available to testers
Analytics dashboard showing usage patterns
Feedback collected and analyzed
Iteration plan based on beta feedback

phases.name: Production Launch Preparation
objectives: Finalize app for production release
Set up production infrastructure and monitoring
Prepare app store listings and marketing materials

infrastructure: Production backend deployment (Vercel, Railway, or AWS)

features: Database backups and disaster recovery plan
Monitoring and alerting for backend services
Privacy policy and terms of service
App icons and splash screens finalized
deliverables: Production infrastructure deployed and tested
App store listings submitted for review
Monitoring and alerting configured
Launch checklist completed

phases.name: Post-Launch Support & Iteration
objectives: Monitor production stability and performance
Address critical bugs and user feedback
Plan and prioritize future features

support: Customer support platform (Intercom, Zendesk, or email)

deliverables: Stable production app with <1% crash rate
User support SLA defined and met
Roadmap for next features (e.g., web dashboard, multi-device support)
Monthly release cadence established

cross_cutting_concerns.accessibility: Ensure WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for all UI components, screen reader support, and sufficient color contrast
cross_cutting_concerns.internationalization: Design with i18n in mind for future multi-language support (use i18next or similar)
cross_cutting_concerns.analytics: Track key metrics: DAU/MAU, message send rate, media upload success rate (original quality), original vs compressed downloads, public share link generation/usage, retention, invite conversion, storage usage per user/chat, subscription conversion rate, free tier limit hits, churn rate
cross_cutting_concerns.compliance: GDPR and CCPA compliance for data privacy, user data export/deletion capabilities, public share link privacy controls
cross_cutting_concerns.documentation: Maintain API documentation, architecture decision records (ADRs), and developer onboarding guides
cross_cutting_concerns.media_quality: Preserve original quality for all uploads (photos, videos, audio), generate compressed versions only for local playback, educate users about storage of precious memories

infrastructure_recommendations.backend_hosting: Vercel (for serverless API), Railway, Render, or AWS ECS/Fargate
infrastructure_recommendations.media_storage: Cloudflare R2 with CDN for faster global access - CRITICAL: Store original full-quality media without compression, separate buckets/prefixes for original vs compressed versions
infrastructure_recommendations.storage_strategy: Two-tier storage: (1) Original quality in R2 for long-term archival, (2) Compressed versions in R2 for app delivery and playback. Consider R2 storage classes for cost optimization.
infrastructure_recommendations.cdn: Cloudflare CDN for serving compressed media globally with low latency, presigned URLs for original quality downloads
infrastructure_recommendations.monitoring: Sentry for error tracking, Datadog or New Relic for APM, R2 storage metrics dashboard for capacity planning
infrastructure_recommendations.ci_cd: GitHub Actions for automated testing and deployment
infrastructure_recommendations.secrets_management: Environment variables via hosting platform, or AWS Secrets Manager/HashiCorp Vault
infrastructure_recommendations.backup_strategy: Regular database backups to separate storage, consider R2 cross-region replication for critical media durability

risk_1.risk: Media upload failures on poor network conditions
risk_1.mitigation: Implement chunked uploads with resume capability, aggressive retry logic, and user feedback

risk_2.risk: Scaling issues with real-time sync for many concurrent users
risk_2.mitigation: Use WebSocket connection pooling, implement rate limiting, consider message broker (Redis Pub/Sub) if needed

risk_3.risk: R2 storage costs escalate with high-quality media volume (storing both original and compressed versions)
risk_3.mitigation: Monitor storage usage closely with alerts, implement storage quotas per tier (500MB free, unlimited premium), use RevenueCat subscriptions to monetize storage, optimize compressed versions aggressively while preserving originals, implement storage analytics dashboard, consider lifecycle policies for rarely-accessed originals (move to cheaper storage class)

risk_3a.risk: Low conversion rate from free to premium tier
risk_3a.mitigation: Implement strategic paywall placement, offer generous free tier to build habit, use RevenueCat experiments for A/B testing pricing, provide clear value proposition for premium, offer trial periods, send upgrade prompts at key moments (limit reached, high engagement)

risk_4.mitigation: Use established libraries (FCM/APNs), implement fallback polling, test extensively on real devices

risk_5.mitigation: Implement rate limiting on invite creation, expiration times, usage limits, and abuse reporting

risk_6.risk: Public share links abused or media leaked
risk_6.mitigation: Short expiration times (max 24 hours), require authentication for share link generation, track access patterns, implement abuse reporting, allow immediate revocation, no indexing by search engines (noindex meta tags)

risk_7.risk: Large original media files cause slow uploads/downloads on mobile networks
risk_7.mitigation: Implement chunked/resumable uploads (multipart), show clear progress indicators, allow background uploads, compress aggressively for local playback, educate users about WiFi recommendations for large files, implement upload size warnings

risk_8.mitigation: 30-day soft-delete retention window, clear restore UI, confirmation dialogs for deletions, consider 'archive' feature as alternative to delete, educate users about cloud backup benefits
